title: Londonderry, Vermont

infobox.value: is a [town](/wiki/New_England_town "New England town") in [Windham County](/wiki/Windham_County,_Vermont "Windham County, Vermont"), [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont"), United States. It is bounded on the north by Landgrove and Weston, on the east by Windham, on the south by Jamaica and on the west by Winhall and Landgrove. The population was 1,919 in the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_Census "2020 United States Census").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The town contains the villages of [Londonderry](/wiki/Londonderry_(CDP),_Vermont "Londonderry (CDP), Vermont") and [South Londonderry](/wiki/South_Londonderry,_Vermont "South Londonderry, Vermont").

**Londonderry** is a [town](/wiki/New_England_town "New England town") in [Windham County](/wiki/Windham_County,_Vermont "Windham County, Vermont"), [Vermont](/wiki/Vermont "Vermont"), United States. It is bounded on the north by Landgrove and Weston, on the east by Windham, on the south by Jamaica and on the west by Winhall and Landgrove. The population was 1,919 in the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_Census "2020 United States Census").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The town contains the villages of [Londonderry](/wiki/Londonderry_(CDP),_Vermont "Londonderry (CDP), Vermont") and [South Londonderry](/wiki/South_Londonderry,_Vermont "South Londonderry, Vermont").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Londonderry,_Vermont&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Londonderry was first chartered in 1770 to a Col. James Rogers and was known as Kent, which included what is now Windham. The town was again chartered by act of the new [Vermont Legislature](/wiki/Vermont_Legislature "Vermont Legislature") on April 20, 1780. In this charter, the township was named Londonderry after [Londonderry](/wiki/Londonderry,_New_Hampshire "Londonderry, New Hampshire") in New Hampshire,[[4]](#cite_note-4) which in turn was named after [Londonderry](/wiki/Derry "Derry") in Ireland.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Londonderry,_Vermont&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the town has a total area of 35.9 square miles (92.9 km2), of which 35.7 square miles (92.4 km2) is land and 0.2 square mile (0.5 km2) (0.59%) is water. The [West River](/wiki/West_River_(Vermont) "West River (Vermont)") flows through the town.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Londonderry,_Vermont&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

This [climatic](/wiki/Climate "Climate") region is typified by large seasonal temperature differences, with warm to hot (and often humid) summers and cold (sometimes severely cold) winters. According to the [Köppen Climate Classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_Climate_Classification "Köppen Climate Classification") system, Londonderry has a [humid continental climate](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate"), abbreviated "Dfb" on climate maps.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Londonderry,_Vermont&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[1]](#cite_note-GR2-1) of 2000, there were 1,709 people, 730 households, and 470 families residing in the town. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 47.9 people per square mile (18.5/km2). There were 1,317 housing units at an average density of 36.9 per square mile (14.3/km2). The racial makeup of the town was 98.60% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.12% Black or African American, 0.23% [American Indian or Alaska Native](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.47% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), and 0.59% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 0.47% of the population.

There were 730 households, out of which 27.7% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 53.3% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 8.1% had a female householder with no husband present, and 35.5% were non-families. Households made up of individuals comprised 25.9% of the population, with 10.7% having someone 65 years of age or older living alone. The average household size was 2.34 and the average family size was 2.84.

In the town, the population's age was spread out, with 21.9% under the age of 18, 6.0% from 18 to 24, 28.7% from 25 to 44, 26.9% from 45 to 64, and 16.6% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 42 years. For every 100 females, there were 102.2 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 103.2 males.

The median income for a household in the town was $42,069, and the median income for a family was $48,000. Males had a median income of $29,871 versus $22,574 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the town was $24,220. About 2.7% of families and 6.3% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 9.2% of those under age 18 and 6.7% of those age 65 or over.
